toolUseResult.content:
# Cartograph + Visioning + Decomposition — Synthesized Design

> Working synthesis intended for end-to-end reading. Pulls together the
> design state from the `c-moa42rxh` (vision-keeper / surveyor) and
> `c-mod99ris` (Reckoner) click subtrees, the `c-mojeikyh` disk-authoring
> decision, and the actual built state of the framework. Goal: read once,
> dispatch what's solid, and rebuild a cleaner click tree against what
> remains open.

---

## Document scope

This doc covers the **patron-vision authoring loop and its decomposition
cascade**: how a patron expresses a long-lived product vision, how the
guild snapshots and tracks it, how that vision gets broken down into
charges and pieces, and how the resulting structure feeds the existing
mandate-dispatch pipeline. It includes:

- The Cartograph data layer (already shipped)
- The disk-authoring flow (designed, not built)
- The decomposition cascade — how a rig examines a cartograph node and
  produces the next layer (designed, not built)
- The Reckoner gating that schedules decomposition rigs alongside other
  guild work (substrate shipped; consumer integration not built)

Out of scope: the existing `vision-keeper` plugin (a Reckoner contract
worked-example — different concern, see §3.3), the codex-Surveyor
apparatus (also out of scope; relevant only for the §6 naming
collision).

---

## 1. The picture

### 1.1 End-to-end flow

```
PATRON
  │ writes vision.md + sidecar in <GUILD>/vision/<slug>/
  │
  │ runs `nsg vision apply <slug>`
  ▼
CARTOGRAPH (data layer)
  │ creates or updates the vision writ + VisionDoc
  │ emits Stacks CDC: book.cartograph.visions.{created,updated}
  ▼
DECOMPOSER-APPARATUS substrate (new)
  │ CDC observer fires
  │ creates a decomposition writ in `new` phase, parentId = the vision
  │ stamps ext.reckoner via reckoner.petition(writId, ext)
  ▼
RECKONER (existing)
  │ holds petition, evaluates per tick
  │ dependency-aware gating, scheduler decides accept/decline/defer
  │ on accept: clerk.transition → decomposition writ moves to `open`
  ▼
SPIDER (existing, extended to dispatch new writ types)
  │ resolves rig template for the decomposition writ
  │ dispatches to the registered decomposer's rig
  ▼
DECOMPOSER RIG (new — provided by the decomposer implementation)
  │ reads the parent (vision) via writ.parentId
  │ reasons about decomposition (capability/gap/risk/journey/etc.)
  │ creates child charges via cartograph API
  │ writes its notes into writ.body
  │ completes the decomposition writ
  ▼
[CASCADE]
  │ each new charge is a new cartograph node → CDC fires →
  │ decomposer-apparatus creates a charge-decomposition writ → recursion
  ▼
LEAF (mandate)
  │ pieces eventually elaborate to mandates
  │ mandates dispatch through the existing implementer pipeline
```

### 1.2 Concept map

| Concept | What it is | Where it lives |
|---|---|---|
| **Vision** | Long-lived patron intent. Authored as `vision.md` + sidecar; snapshotted into a writ + VisionDoc. | On disk + cartograph |
| **Charge** | First decomposition under a vision. Patron-contract boundary. The unit of patron walkthrough. Does NOT self-nest. | Cartograph |
| **Piece** | Recursive internal organization under a charge. Self-nests. No patron contract. | Cartograph |
| **Mandate** | Existing leaf writ, attaches at any depth under a charge. Dispatched by Spider as today. | Clerk |
| **Decomposition writ** | A typed writ that carries a rig's act of decomposing one cartograph node. Body holds the rig's notes. Three flavors (one per cartograph layer) or one with a layer field — see §6.2. | New, owned by decomposer-apparatus |
| **Decomposer** | The role/agent that performs decomposition. Emits petitions to the Reckoner via the apparatus's CDC observer. | New |
| **Decomposer-apparatus** | Substrate plugin that owns decomposition writ types, the CDC observer, the rig-name convention, and the registry of contributed decomposer implementations. | New |
| **Default decomposer** | The first concrete decomposer registered with the substrate. Provides minimal rig templates per layer. | New |

> **Naming note.** I'm using "decomposer" as a placeholder throughout
> this document. We just renamed everything to "surveyor / surveying" —
> but the framework already reserves **Surveyor** for the
> codex-awareness apparatus (see §6.1 for the collision and options).
> Treat "decomposer" as a stand-in until the naming gets resolved.

---

## 2. State of the system — what's shipped

### 2.1 Summary table

| Component | Package | Key commits | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| Cartograph data layer (vision/charge/piece writ types + companion docs) | `@shardworks/cartograph-apparatus` | `f69f1c6` (scaffold) | **Shipped** |
| Cartograph CLI (vision/charge/piece × create/show/list/patch/transition) | (in cartograph-apparatus) | `a04cd6c` | **Shipped** |
| Reckoner core (registry, source-id grammar, ext slot, petition/withdraw helpers) | `@shardworks/reckoner-apparatus` | `89fb1ab`, `64113e0`, `f7fb5b0` | **Shipped** |
| Reckoner CDC handler + Reckonings book + startup catch-up | (reckoner) | `e59abd6` | **Shipped** |
| Reckoner scheduler kit-contribution (always-approve default) | (reckoner) | `d068b65` | **Shipped** |
| Reckoner periodic tick (replaces CDC handler) | (reckoner) | `7bed456` | **Shipped** |
| Reckoner dependency-aware consideration gate | (reckoner) | `0e1e81f` | **Shipped** |
| `depends-on` link kind (Clerk-contributed, was `spider.follows`) | clerk + spider | `5f4a0f1` | **Shipped** |
| Vision-keeper worked-example petitioner (NOT a decomposer — see §2.4) | `@shardworks/vision-keeper-apparatus` | `4147d4f`, `b92dc90` | **Shipped** |

### 2.2 The Cartograph

The Cartograph apparatus contributes three writ types — `vision`, `charge`,
`piece` — each backed by a typed companion doc keyed by writ id.

```typescript
interface VisionDoc { id: string; stage: VisionStage; codex?: string; createdAt: string; updatedAt: string; }
interface ChargeDoc { id: string; stage: ChargeStage; ... }
interface PieceDoc  { id: string; stage: PieceStage;  ... }

type VisionStage = 'draft' | 'active' | 'sunset' | 'cancelled';
type ChargeStage = 'draft' | 'active' | 'validated' | 'dropped';
type PieceStage  = 'draft' | 'active' | 'done' | 'dropped';
```

The `CartographApi` enforces ladder invariants (vision has no parent;
charge.parentId must be vision; piece.parentId must be charge or piece)
at the typed-API surface. Raw `clerk.post()` is intentionally
unconstrained — the typed API is the contract.

The CLI exposes `nsg vision/charge/piece × create/show/list/patch/transition`,
all `callableBy: ['patron']`. Creates land at `phase: new` / `stage: draft`
with no auto-transition.

**What the Cartograph does NOT own:** decomposition, rigs, anything that
turns one node into children. It is a pure data layer.

### 2.3 The Reckoner

The Reckoner is the petition scheduler. The relevant surface for this
design:

- `petition(request)` — create a writ + stamp `ext.reckoner` (Workflow 2)
- `petition(writId, extRequest)` — stamp-only form for the
  draft-then-publish idiom (`f7fb5b0`)
- `withdraw(writId, reason)` — cancel a held petition
- Periodic tick (`@every 60s`) re-evaluates held petitions
- Dependency-aware consideration: outbound `depends-on` links to
  non-terminal writs cause `defer` with reason
  `dependency_pending|dependency_failed`
- Scheduler kit contribution: pluggable scheduler picks accept/decline/defer

For the decomposition cascade, the Reckoner is the gate that schedules
decomposition rigs alongside other guild work (implementation rigs,
tech-debt watchers, etc.). It is consumer-ready.

### 2.4 The Vision-keeper plugin (worked example, not a decomposer)

> ⚠️ **Conceptual landmine.** The shipped `vision-keeper` plugin is
> NOT what this document is designing. It exists as the canonical
> Reckoner-contract worked example: it submits drift snapshots and
> elaboration nudges to demonstrate every petitioner contract surface.
> It does not survey, decompose, or run rigs. It registers the
> `vision-keeper.snapshot` source and emits petitions; that's its
> entire job.

When Sean's c-moiu7yc1 conclusion proposed splitting vision-keeper into
a "vision-keeper-apparatus" with rig runtime, that proposal was about
a *future* apparatus that would stand alongside the existing worked
example. The decomposer-apparatus we're designing is conceptually that
future apparatus. The existing vision-keeper plugin should probably be
renamed (e.g., `vision-keeper-example` or `drift-petitioner-example`)
to dissolve the confusion — but that's a sanctum-side cleanup, not part
of this design.

---

## 3. State of the system — what's in flight

Nothing currently in flight in this domain. The two open Reckoner
commissions from yesterday's design burst (periodic tick, dependency-
aware consideration) both sealed (commits `7bed456`, `0e1e81f`).

---

## 4. State of the system — what hasn't been built

The entire decomposition-cascade machinery:

- Disk-authored visions (`<GUILD>/vision/<slug>/vision.md` + sidecar)
- `nsg vision apply` CLI verb
- Decomposition writ types (`decomp-vision` / `decomp-charge` / `decomp-piece`,
  or one `decomp` type with a layer field — TBD)
- DecompDoc companion type and `books.decomps` (or whatever the type is named)
- Decomposer-apparatus substrate plugin (CDC observer, registry, rig-name
  convention, doc stamping)
- Default decomposer plugin (rig templates per layer)
- Spider extension: dispatch decomposition writ types in addition to
  mandates
- `vision-keeper.md` is a placeholder stub in the cartograph package

---

## 5. Solid design — ready to commission

These design decisions are settled across the click tree. Each could
be commissioned today (provided naming questions in §6.1 are resolved).

### 5.1 Vision authoring on disk (`c-mojeikyh`)

The patron's authoring surface is a directory tree, not a CLI:

```
<GUILD>/vision/
  <slug>/
    vision.md             ← prose, patron-edited
    vision-metadata.yml   ← sidecar; carries `visionId` after first apply
```

- The slug is a directory affordance, not a durable identity. After
  first apply, the sidecar's `visionId` field binds the file tree to
  its cartograph writ — the only durable identity, written by the
  system, never edited by hand.
- `nsg vision apply <slug>` is a single code path for both first import
  and Nth re-import: locate sidecar, resolve bound writ via `visionId`
  (or create new), copy prose into `writ.body`, sync stage/codex via
  `transitionVision`, and on first run write the new id back into the
  sidecar.
- Data flow is **one-way: file → writ**. The writ is a snapshot of
  patron intent; the file is the editable source.
- Stale-binding (file deleted, writ remains): warn at scan, error on
  next apply attempt; patron transitions writ to sunset/cancelled
  explicitly.
- Future shape: fs-watcher / git-hook auto-trigger of apply, drift
  detection between writ snapshot and file content. Out of scope for
  v0.

This subsumes the earlier "initial petition timing" and "periodic
re-evaluation" design questions: every apply creates the same Stacks
CDC stream (creates and updates), and the substrate's observer is
agnostic to whether this is the 1st or Nth import.

### 5.2 Decomposition is a typed-writ-driven cascade

Decomposition is its own first-class activity. It is NOT a mandate
variant. The cascade has a uniform shape at every layer:

1. A cartograph node lands (vision via apply, charge or piece via
   upstream rig).
2. CDC fires on the cartograph book — `created` or `updated`.
3. The decomposer-apparatus's observer creates a decomposition writ in
   `new` phase, with `parentId` set to the cartograph node.
4. The observer stamps `ext.reckoner` to enter the petition queue.
5. Reckoner gates per its existing rules.
6. On accept (transition to `open`), Spider dispatches the decomposer's
   rig.
7. Rig reads the parent, decomposes, creates children via cartograph
   API, writes notes, completes the writ.
8. Each child cartograph node fires a fresh CDC event → loop to step 2
   one layer down.

This is the recursive-cascade pattern from `c-moiu7yc1`, except the
dispatchable unit is now the decomposition writ (typed), not a
trivial-bodied mandate.

### 5.3 Decomposition writs carry the rig's notes in `writ.body`

Decomposition writs naturally fit the ladder-writ pattern, not the
mandate pattern:

| Pattern | `writ.body` is... | Set when |
|---|---|---|
| Mandate | the brief (instructions to the rig) | creation |
| Vision/charge/piece | the canonical content (prose, the thing itself) | creation/apply |
| Decomposition writ | the rig's notes (canonical content of the act of decomposing) | rig completion |

The decomposition writ IS the examination performed. Its content IS
the examiner's notes — the reasoning trace, the considered alternatives,
the rationale for the structural decisions made. Notes lives in
`writ.body`; the rig writes them on completion. Pre-completion, body
is empty/placeholder.

This dissolves the "kinda sucks — body is just `decompose vis-X-123`"
concern. The body becomes the substantive output of the rig, not a
trivial pointer.

### 5.4 Companion `DecompDoc` holds envelope metadata only

Following the cartograph precedent (VisionDoc/ChargeDoc/PieceDoc),
each decomposition writ has a typed companion doc keyed by writ id:

```typescript
interface DecompDoc {
  id: string;             // primary key — the decomposition writ id
  targetNodeId: string;   // the cartograph node being decomposed
  rigName: string;        // 'decomp-vision' | 'decomp-charge' | 'decomp-piece'
  rigVersion: string;     // for replay / diffing across rig versions
  decomposerId: string;   // which decomposer ran ('scaffold-decomposer' etc.)
  completedAt: string;    // ISO timestamp
  // notes are NOT here — they live in writ.body
}
```

Owned by the decomposer-apparatus substrate; shared across all
decomposer implementations. This is a deliberate narrowing from the
earlier design where the doc carried both metadata and notes — moving
notes to `writ.body` makes the writ first-class content and lets the
doc shrink to envelope.

### 5.5 Substrate + extension + default plugin shape

Three plugins, mirroring the Reckoner pattern:

1. **`@shardworks/cartograph-apparatus`** *(shipped)* — pure data layer.
   Vision/charge/piece writ types + companion docs + ladder-invariant
   API. Does not own any decomposition concept.

2. **`@shardworks/<decomposer>-apparatus`** *(new — substrate)*
   - Owns decomposition writ types and `books.<decomps>`
   - Owns the decomposer registry (kit-contribution surface)
   - Owns the CDC observer that fires on cartograph creates+updates
   - Owns the rig-name convention (`decomp-vision/charge/piece`)
   - Routes accepted decomposition petitions to the registered
     decomposer's rig templates
   - Stamps DecompDoc on completion (rig fills `writ.body`; substrate
     wraps the writ)
   - Provides the decomposer role-file convention
   - **Does not ship a concrete decomposer.** Substrate-only.

3. **`@shardworks/scaffold-<decomposer>`** *(new — default)*
   - Registers a decomposer with the substrate via kit contribution
   - Provides minimal rig templates for each cartograph layer
   - Useful immediately; designed to be replaced as approaches are
     iterated

Decomposing is implementation-uncertain territory — Sean has explicitly
said "we will need to experiment with approaches." The substrate
captures durable mechanics; the extension lets implementations evolve
freely.

### 5.6 Substrate watches cartograph CDC

The substrate's observer subscribes to:

- `book.cartograph.visions.created`
- `book.cartograph.visions.updated`
- `book.cartograph.charges.created`
- `book.cartograph.charges.updated`
- `book.cartograph.pieces.created`
- `book.cartograph.pieces.updated`

On any event, it creates the appropriate decomposition writ with
`parentId` pointing to the affected cartograph node, and stamps
`ext.reckoner` to enter the petition queue. The rig itself decides
whether the change warrants new structure — a typo-fix re-apply can
result in a decomposition rig whose notes say "no change warranted; no
children created."

Whether to gate on stage transitions (only fire when `stage: active`)
is open — see §6.2.

### 5.7 Spider's dispatchable type set extends

`c-moiu7yc1` originally concluded "mandates remain the dispatchable
unit (no need to extend dispatch to non-mandate writ types, even
though Spider's rigTemplateMappings would compose with that)." This
design needs to extend that — Spider's dispatchable set grows to
include decomposition writ types. The original conclusion explicitly
noted this would compose; the only reason to keep mandates as the sole
dispatchable type was "no need yet."

---

## 6. Open questions

### 6.1 Naming — the Surveyor collision

**This is the load-bearing question that gates several others.**

The recent rename (this conversation) renamed:
- `review-*` rigs → `survey-*` rigs
- `vision-keeper` role → `surveyor`
- The activity → "surveying"
- The substrate plugin → `surveyor-apparatus`
- The default → `scaffold-surveyor`

But the framework architecture **already reserves "Surveyor"** for the
codex-awareness apparatus. From `docs/guild-metaphor.md`:

> The Surveyor maintains the guild's knowledge of its codexes. When a
> codex is registered, the Surveyor inspects it — determining what
> kinds of work are applicable and how each is fulfilled for that
> specific codex.

And from `docs/architecture/index.md` line 27: the Surveyor is named
alongside Clerk and Clockworks as a core apparatus. From line 286: it
is "described as part of the guild's operational fabric, but [is] not
yet extracted as a standalone package."

**The collision is real.** The codex-Surveyor and our
cartograph-decomposer are conceptually adjacent (both produce
"knowledge of work-applicability over a substrate") but operate on
different substrates. Squinting at them as one apparatus blurs two
distinct concerns.

**Options:**

| Option | Move |
|---|---|
| (a) Keep "surveyor" for our thing; rename the codex-Surveyor (e.g., `Cartomancer`, `Lookout`, `Scout`) | Touches the architecture's reserved vocabulary; bigger blast radius |
| (b) Rename our thing back; leave codex-Surveyor as canonical | Re-rename right after the previous rename (churn) |
| (c) Disambiguate with prefixes (codex-surveyor, cartograph-surveyor) | Verbose but unambiguous |
| (d) Unify both under one Surveyor apparatus with multiple "domains" | Conflates two concerns; unclear win |

**My read:** (b) is the least bad. The codex-Surveyor concept is older
and load-bearing in the architecture; our naming pass landed in less
than a session and the alias registry already documents the rename so
re-renaming is cheap. The next-best name for our thing pairs with
`cartograph` directly: **Cartographer** (role) / **cartography**
(activity) / **chart** (output). The cartographer works the cartograph.

Rig names under "cartographer":
- `chart-vision`, `chart-charge`, `chart-piece` — short; the
  `chart-charge` c-c repetition Sean flagged earlier is real but
  livable
- `cartograph-vision/charge/piece` — verbose but unambiguous
- `decompose-vision/charge/piece` — bland, no register fit

**Sean's call. Until resolved, this doc uses "decomposer" /
"decomposition" as placeholders to avoid further entrenching either
name.**

### 6.2 Stage gating — should drafts trigger decomposition?

A vision lands at `stage: draft` per the cartograph CLI convention.
Current `nsg vision apply` design (per §5.1) presumably activates the
vision in the same call (or stage transitions are handled in the
sidecar). Two questions:

1. Should the substrate's observer fire on `stage: draft` events, or
   only on transitions to `stage: active`?
2. If gated on stage, what's the exact trigger — a stage transition
   event, or "stage is active at the moment we observe a create/update"?

**v0 lean:** every apply triggers a decomposition. If the patron wants
to draft without decomposing, they don't apply yet. Stage-gating layers
on later when draft-without-decomposing becomes a real workflow need.

**Risk:** patron does a series of edits + applies during initial
authoring; each apply triggers a decomposition petition. Reckoner
handles the queue but resource costs accrue. Maybe we want the
stage-active gate from day one.

### 6.3 Dynamic rig template registration (`c-moje41iq`)

Spider currently resolves rigs via static `rigTemplateMappings`
configured by writ type. Concrete decomposer implementations need to
register rig templates for `decomp-vision/charge/piece` (or whatever
the typed names become) at plugin load time.

Four candidate mechanisms:

1. **Dynamic kit-contribution registration** — plugin's `supportKit`
   declares rig templates; Spider aggregates static config + contributed
   templates at startup. Matches the Reckoner-scheduler pattern. **Most
   natural fit.**
2. **Static config with templating** — parameter substitution. Poor
   fit; decomposers want fundamentally different rigs, not parameter
   variations.
3. **Rig 'include' composition** — useful for shared substrate
   boilerplate (DecompDoc stamping, parent-reading) but doesn't solve
   the registration question on its own.
4. **Engine grafting / graftTailing** — substrate provides one universal
   decomposition rig; decomposer contributes engines that get grafted at
   runtime. Higher substrate share; more complex.

**Prerequisite:** audit Spider's existing rig-resolution mechanism. We
need to know how it discriminates between rigs when multiple writ
"kinds" exist (implementer/spec/reviewer/decomposer would all
potentially be different rigs over different writ types). The
dispatchable-set extension from §5.7 needs this audit before it can be
designed concretely.

### 6.4 Default `scaffold-<decomposer>` design (`c-moje4dj9`)

What does the default decomposer actually do?

- One rig template per cartograph layer, or one recursive template that
  handles all three?
- Model selection (sonnet vs opus vs other)?
- Prompt structure — what does the rig actually read and write?
- Stop conditions: when does the rig decide to stop creating children
  vs decompose further?
- Re-decomposition (drift) — does the rig know it's a re-run vs first
  pass? Does it diff against prior decomposition writs for the same
  target node?

**v0 lean:** three layer-specific templates (matches Spider's
per-writ-type dispatch model from the audit); basic LLM prompts; no
stop conditions other than "create children if you think the parent
warrants further breakdown, otherwise complete with notes explaining
why no children were warranted"; re-runs are stateless (rig reads
prior decomposition writs as context if it wants).

### 6.5 Substrate scope detail (`c-moje49pk`)

Where exactly does the substrate / decomposer-implementation boundary
fall?

| Capability | Substrate? | Decomposer impl? |
|---|---|---|
| CDC observer for cartograph book changes | Yes | No |
| Initial decomposition petition emission | Yes | No |
| Rig-template routing (writ type → template id) | Yes (registry-based) | No |
| Actual rig template (prompts, model, tools) | No | Yes |
| DecompDoc stamping on rig completion | Yes | No |
| Default priority-dimension scheme | Yes (overridable) | Optional override |
| Stop conditions / decomposition heuristics | No | Yes |
| Source-id grammar for ext.reckoner | Yes (default) | Optional override |
| Role-file convention | Yes | Optional override |

**Goal:** push as much as possible into substrate so concrete
decomposers are minimal kit contributions. This table is a starting
proposal — Sean's review/revision welcome.

### 6.6 Plural decomposers (`c-moje4foc`, currently parked)

When multiple decomposer implementations are registered with the
substrate, how does the substrate select which one to dispatch for a
given decomposition petition? Per-vision config? Per-cartograph-tree?
Round-robin? Default-with-override?

**Not for v0** — only one decomposer registered. Parked.

---

## 7. Recommended commission sequence

These can dispatch as soon as naming (§6.1) is resolved. Each is small
enough to commission as a single brief.

### Commission A — Vision authoring on disk

- `nsg vision apply <slug>` CLI verb (single code path for first/Nth
  import)
- Sidecar schema (`vision-metadata.yml` with `visionId`, stage, codex)
- Stale-binding detection (warn at scan, error on apply)
- Tests: import a fresh vision, re-import after edits, error on stale
  binding

Ships in the cartograph-apparatus package; no new plugin required.

### Commission B — Decomposer-apparatus substrate

- New plugin `@shardworks/<decomposer>-apparatus`
- Decomposition writ types (one or three — settle in commission brief
  per §6.4)
- DecompDoc + `books.<decomps>` companion book
- Decomposer registry (kit-contribution surface)
- CDC observer on cartograph book events; creates decomposition writs
- Rig-template routing (substrate looks up registered decomposer's
  templates)
- DecompDoc stamping on rig completion
- Tests: CDC fires → writ created → ext.reckoner stamped

Depends on resolution of §6.3 (dynamic rig-template registration) for
the routing piece — that may need a small Spider PR first.

### Commission C — Spider extension for decomposition writ dispatch

- Audit Spider's current rig-resolution mechanism (§6.3 prerequisite)
- Extend dispatchable-type set to include decomposition writ types
- Template lookup integrates with the substrate's registry
- Tests: a typed decomposition writ in `open` phase dispatches the
  registered decomposer's rig

Could fold into Commission B or stand alone. Standalone if the audit
reveals Spider work that's worth scoping separately.

### Commission D — Scaffold-decomposer default

- New plugin `@shardworks/scaffold-<decomposer>`
- Three rig templates (vision, charge, piece) — basic LLM prompts
- Registers with the substrate via kit contribution
- Tests: end-to-end smoke test (vision applied → decomposition writ
  created → rig dispatched → charges created → recursion fires)

Depends on B (substrate exists) and C (Spider can dispatch). This is
the first-light commission for the whole cascade.

### Order of operations

```
A (vision apply) ─┐
                  ├─→ E2E ready
B (substrate) ────┤
C (Spider) ───────┤
D (default) ──────┘
```

A is independent of the others. B/C/D have dependencies as written. A
clean dispatch order: A first (it's smallest and ships value
immediately even before decomposition lands), then B+C in either order
(small audit needed for C), then D last.

---

## 8. Proposed click tree (replacing c-moa42rxh subtree)

The current `c-moa42rxh` subtree has 22 children, many concluded but
some live with overlapping concerns. After this synthesis lands, a
cleaner tree could look like:

```
c-NEW-ROOT  Decomposition cascade — patron-vision authoring + cartograph
            decomposition + Reckoner-gated dispatch [live]
├── c-NEW-A  Vision authoring on disk (commission A)               [live]
├── c-NEW-B  Decomposer-apparatus substrate (commission B)         [live]
│   ├── c-NEW-B1  Substrate scope detail (was c-moje49pk)          [live]
│   ├── c-NEW-B2  Stage gating policy (§6.2)                       [live]
│   └── c-NEW-B3  Decomposition writ shape — one type or three     [live]
├── c-NEW-C  Spider rig-resolution audit + extension (commission C) [live]
│   └── c-NEW-C1  Dynamic rig-template registration (was c-moje41iq) [live]
├── c-NEW-D  Default scaffold-decomposer (commission D)            [live]
│   └── c-NEW-D1  Rig template shape per layer (was c-moje4dj9)    [live]
├── c-NEW-Z  Naming — Surveyor collision (§6.1, gates everything)  [live]
└── c-NEW-Y  Plural decomposers — future                           [parked]

[Concluded historical clicks parked under an "archive" branch:
 c-mod53o6h (cartograph data scaffold + 5 children, all shipped),
 c-mod53ood, c-mod53q2r, c-modee*, c-moeplgm5, c-moerc0vf,
 c-moivk7pd subtree, c-moivk8yc, c-moivk9qw, c-moivkc4y,
 c-moivkfgb, c-moiu7yc1, c-moiu8pm9, c-moiu8tm4, c-moje45l8,
 c-moje41iq, c-moje49pk, c-moje4dj9, c-moje4foc]
```

The reorg moves from "design questions about a vision-keeper concept"
to "commissions and their open questions." The old design questions
either resolved (concluded) or got absorbed into commission scope.

---

## Appendix: source clicks

The clicks this synthesis draws from. Concluded conclusions are the
authoritative record; live clicks are open design.

**Concluded (load-bearing decisions):**

- `c-mod53o6h` — Product as first-class writ type; cartograph scaffold
- `c-mod53ood` — Ladder shape (vision/charge/piece/mandate)
- `c-modee576` — Companion-book pattern keyed by writ id
- `c-modee5kb` — Parent/child via writ.parentId
- `c-modee5x4` — Ladder invariants enforced at typed-API surface
- `c-modeor2d` — Naming finalized (vision/charge/piece, XxxDoc convention)
- `c-moeplgm5` — Cartograph scaffold dispatched + sealed
- `c-moerc0vf` — Cartograph CLI dispatched + sealed

  (now superseded by §5.7 in this doc)

  WIP-cap deadlock)
- `c-moiwnb9i` — `reckoner.petition()` stamp-only overload
- `c-moiwnmoc` — Reckoner dependency-aware consideration
- `c-moiwnzw6` — `spider.follows` → `depends-on` rename

  by §5.3 — notes move to writ.body)

  superseding the whole subtree with the c-NEW-ROOT shape)

**Parked:**

content.text: One important discovery before drafting — the framework architecture already reserves "Surveyor" for the codex-awareness apparatus (per `guild-metaphor.md` and `architecture/index.md`). The recent rename we landed creates a naming collision. I'll flag it crisply in the doc.

input.content:
# Cartograph + Visioning + Decomposition — Synthesized Design

> Working synthesis intended for end-to-end reading. Pulls together the
> design state from the `c-moa42rxh` (vision-keeper / surveyor) and
> `c-mod99ris` (Reckoner) click subtrees, the `c-mojeikyh` disk-authoring
> decision, and the actual built state of the framework. Goal: read once,
> dispatch what's solid, and rebuild a cleaner click tree against what
> remains open.

---

## Document scope

This doc covers the **patron-vision authoring loop and its decomposition
cascade**: how a patron expresses a long-lived product vision, how the
guild snapshots and tracks it, how that vision gets broken down into
charges and pieces, and how the resulting structure feeds the existing
mandate-dispatch pipeline. It includes:

- The Cartograph data layer (already shipped)
- The disk-authoring flow (designed, not built)
- The decomposition cascade — how a rig examines a cartograph node and
  produces the next layer (designed, not built)
- The Reckoner gating that schedules decomposition rigs alongside other
  guild work (substrate shipped; consumer integration not built)

Out of scope: the existing `vision-keeper` plugin (a Reckoner contract
worked-example — different concern, see §3.3), the codex-Surveyor
apparatus (also out of scope; relevant only for the §6 naming
collision).

---

## 1. The picture

### 1.1 End-to-end flow

```
PATRON
  │ writes vision.md + sidecar in <GUILD>/vision/<slug>/
  │
  │ runs `nsg vision apply <slug>`
  ▼
CARTOGRAPH (data layer)
  │ creates or updates the vision writ + VisionDoc
  │ emits Stacks CDC: book.cartograph.visions.{created,updated}
  ▼
DECOMPOSER-APPARATUS substrate (new)
  │ CDC observer fires
  │ creates a decomposition writ in `new` phase, parentId = the vision
  │ stamps ext.reckoner via reckoner.petition(writId, ext)
  ▼
RECKONER (existing)
  │ holds petition, evaluates per tick
  │ dependency-aware gating, scheduler decides accept/decline/defer
  │ on accept: clerk.transition → decomposition writ moves to `open`
  ▼
SPIDER (existing, extended to dispatch new writ types)
  │ resolves rig template for the decomposition writ
  │ dispatches to the registered decomposer's rig
  ▼
DECOMPOSER RIG (new — provided by the decomposer implementation)
  │ reads the parent (vision) via writ.parentId
  │ reasons about decomposition (capability/gap/risk/journey/etc.)
  │ creates child charges via cartograph API
  │ writes its notes into writ.body
  │ completes the decomposition writ
  ▼
[CASCADE]
  │ each new charge is a new cartograph node → CDC fires →
  │ decomposer-apparatus creates a charge-decomposition writ → recursion
  ▼
LEAF (mandate)
  │ pieces eventually elaborate to mandates
  │ mandates dispatch through the existing implementer pipeline
```

### 1.2 Concept map

| Concept | What it is | Where it lives |
|---|---|---|
| **Vision** | Long-lived patron intent. Authored as `vision.md` + sidecar; snapshotted into a writ + VisionDoc. | On disk + cartograph |
| **Charge** | First decomposition under a vision. Patron-contract boundary. The unit of patron walkthrough. Does NOT self-nest. | Cartograph |
| **Piece** | Recursive internal organization under a charge. Self-nests. No patron contract. | Cartograph |
| **Mandate** | Existing leaf writ, attaches at any depth under a charge. Dispatched by Spider as today. | Clerk |
| **Decomposition writ** | A typed writ that carries a rig's act of decomposing one cartograph node. Body holds the rig's notes. Three flavors (one per cartograph layer) or one with a layer field — see §6.2. | New, owned by decomposer-apparatus |
| **Decomposer** | The role/agent that performs decomposition. Emits petitions to the Reckoner via the apparatus's CDC observer. | New |
| **Decomposer-apparatus** | Substrate plugin that owns decomposition writ types, the CDC observer, the rig-name convention, and the registry of contributed decomposer implementations. | New |
| **Default decomposer** | The first concrete decomposer registered with the substrate. Provides minimal rig templates per layer. | New |

> **Naming note.** I'm using "decomposer" as a placeholder throughout
> this document. We just renamed everything to "surveyor / surveying" —
> but the framework already reserves **Surveyor** for the
> codex-awareness apparatus (see §6.1 for the collision and options).
> Treat "decomposer" as a stand-in until the naming gets resolved.

---

## 2. State of the system — what's shipped

### 2.1 Summary table

| Component | Package | Key commits | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| Cartograph data layer (vision/charge/piece writ types + companion docs) | `@shardworks/cartograph-apparatus` | `f69f1c6` (scaffold) | **Shipped** |
| Cartograph CLI (vision/charge/piece × create/show/list/patch/transition) | (in cartograph-apparatus) | `a04cd6c` | **Shipped** |
| Reckoner core (registry, source-id grammar, ext slot, petition/withdraw helpers) | `@shardworks/reckoner-apparatus` | `89fb1ab`, `64113e0`, `f7fb5b0` | **Shipped** |
| Reckoner CDC handler + Reckonings book + startup catch-up | (reckoner) | `e59abd6` | **Shipped** |
| Reckoner scheduler kit-contribution (always-approve default) | (reckoner) | `d068b65` | **Shipped** |
| Reckoner periodic tick (replaces CDC handler) | (reckoner) | `7bed456` | **Shipped** |
| Reckoner dependency-aware consideration gate | (reckoner) | `0e1e81f` | **Shipped** |
| `depends-on` link kind (Clerk-contributed, was `spider.follows`) | clerk + spider | `5f4a0f1` | **Shipped** |
| Vision-keeper worked-example petitioner (NOT a decomposer — see §2.4) | `@shardworks/vision-keeper-apparatus` | `4147d4f`, `b92dc90` | **Shipped** |

### 2.2 The Cartograph

The Cartograph apparatus contributes three writ types — `vision`, `charge`,
`piece` — each backed by a typed companion doc keyed by writ id.

```typescript
interface VisionDoc { id: string; stage: VisionStage; codex?: string; createdAt: string; updatedAt: string; }
interface ChargeDoc { id: string; stage: ChargeStage; ... }
interface PieceDoc  { id: string; stage: PieceStage;  ... }

type VisionStage = 'draft' | 'active' | 'sunset' | 'cancelled';
type ChargeStage = 'draft' | 'active' | 'validated' | 'dropped';
type PieceStage  = 'draft' | 'active' | 'done' | 'dropped';
```

The `CartographApi` enforces ladder invariants (vision has no parent;
charge.parentId must be vision; piece.parentId must be charge or piece)
at the typed-API surface. Raw `clerk.post()` is intentionally
unconstrained — the typed API is the contract.

The CLI exposes `nsg vision/charge/piece × create/show/list/patch/transition`,
all `callableBy: ['patron']`. Creates land at `phase: new` / `stage: draft`
with no auto-transition.

**What the Cartograph does NOT own:** decomposition, rigs, anything that
turns one node into children. It is a pure data layer.

### 2.3 The Reckoner

The Reckoner is the petition scheduler. The relevant surface for this
design:

- `petition(request)` — create a writ + stamp `ext.reckoner` (Workflow 2)
- `petition(writId, extRequest)` — stamp-only form for the
  draft-then-publish idiom (`f7fb5b0`)
- `withdraw(writId, reason)` — cancel a held petition
- Periodic tick (`@every 60s`) re-evaluates held petitions
- Dependency-aware consideration: outbound `depends-on` links to
  non-terminal writs cause `defer` with reason
  `dependency_pending|dependency_failed`
- Scheduler kit contribution: pluggable scheduler picks accept/decline/defer

For the decomposition cascade, the Reckoner is the gate that schedules
decomposition rigs alongside other guild work (implementation rigs,
tech-debt watchers, etc.). It is consumer-ready.

### 2.4 The Vision-keeper plugin (worked example, not a decomposer)

> ⚠️ **Conceptual landmine.** The shipped `vision-keeper` plugin is
> NOT what this document is designing. It exists as the canonical
> Reckoner-contract worked example: it submits drift snapshots and
> elaboration nudges to demonstrate every petitioner contract surface.
> It does not survey, decompose, or run rigs. It registers the
> `vision-keeper.snapshot` source and emits petitions; that's its
> entire job.

When Sean's c-moiu7yc1 conclusion proposed splitting vision-keeper into
a "vision-keeper-apparatus" with rig runtime, that proposal was about
a *future* apparatus that would stand alongside the existing worked
example. The decomposer-apparatus we're designing is conceptually that
future apparatus. The existing vision-keeper plugin should probably be
renamed (e.g., `vision-keeper-example` or `drift-petitioner-example`)
to dissolve the confusion — but that's a sanctum-side cleanup, not part
of this design.

---

## 3. State of the system — what's in flight

Nothing currently in flight in this domain. The two open Reckoner
commissions from yesterday's design burst (periodic tick, dependency-
aware consideration) both sealed (commits `7bed456`, `0e1e81f`).

---

## 4. State of the system — what hasn't been built

The entire decomposition-cascade machinery:

- Disk-authored visions (`<GUILD>/vision/<slug>/vision.md` + sidecar)
- `nsg vision apply` CLI verb
- Decomposition writ types (`decomp-vision` / `decomp-charge` / `decomp-piece`,
  or one `decomp` type with a layer field — TBD)
- DecompDoc companion type and `books.decomps` (or whatever the type is named)
- Decomposer-apparatus substrate plugin (CDC observer, registry, rig-name
  convention, doc stamping)
- Default decomposer plugin (rig templates per layer)
- Spider extension: dispatch decomposition writ types in addition to
  mandates
- `vision-keeper.md` is a placeholder stub in the cartograph package

---

## 5. Solid design — ready to commission

These design decisions are settled across the click tree. Each could
be commissioned today (provided naming questions in §6.1 are resolved).

### 5.1 Vision authoring on disk (`c-mojeikyh`)

The patron's authoring surface is a directory tree, not a CLI:

```
<GUILD>/vision/
  <slug>/
    vision.md             ← prose, patron-edited
    vision-metadata.yml   ← sidecar; carries `visionId` after first apply
```

- The slug is a directory affordance, not a durable identity. After
  first apply, the sidecar's `visionId` field binds the file tree to
  its cartograph writ — the only durable identity, written by the
  system, never edited by hand.
- `nsg vision apply <slug>` is a single code path for both first import
  and Nth re-import: locate sidecar, resolve bound writ via `visionId`
  (or create new), copy prose into `writ.body`, sync stage/codex via
  `transitionVision`, and on first run write the new id back into the
  sidecar.
- Data flow is **one-way: file → writ**. The writ is a snapshot of
  patron intent; the file is the editable source.
- Stale-binding (file deleted, writ remains): warn at scan, error on
  next apply attempt; patron transitions writ to sunset/cancelled
  explicitly.
- Future shape: fs-watcher / git-hook auto-trigger of apply, drift
  detection between writ snapshot and file content. Out of scope for
  v0.

This subsumes the earlier "initial petition timing" and "periodic
re-evaluation" design questions: every apply creates the same Stacks
CDC stream (creates and updates), and the substrate's observer is
agnostic to whether this is the 1st or Nth import.

### 5.2 Decomposition is a typed-writ-driven cascade

Decomposition is its own first-class activity. It is NOT a mandate
variant. The cascade has a uniform shape at every layer:

1. A cartograph node lands (vision via apply, charge or piece via
   upstream rig).
2. CDC fires on the cartograph book — `created` or `updated`.
3. The decomposer-apparatus's observer creates a decomposition writ in
   `new` phase, with `parentId` set to the cartograph node.
4. The observer stamps `ext.reckoner` to enter the petition queue.
5. Reckoner gates per its existing rules.
6. On accept (transition to `open`), Spider dispatches the decomposer's
   rig.
7. Rig reads the parent, decomposes, creates children via cartograph
   API, writes notes, completes the writ.
8. Each child cartograph node fires a fresh CDC event → loop to step 2
   one layer down.

This is the recursive-cascade pattern from `c-moiu7yc1`, except the
dispatchable unit is now the decomposition writ (typed), not a
trivial-bodied mandate.

### 5.3 Decomposition writs carry the rig's notes in `writ.body`

Decomposition writs naturally fit the ladder-writ pattern, not the
mandate pattern:

| Pattern | `writ.body` is... | Set when |
|---|---|---|
| Mandate | the brief (instructions to the rig) | creation |
| Vision/charge/piece | the canonical content (prose, the thing itself) | creation/apply |
| Decomposition writ | the rig's notes (canonical content of the act of decomposing) | rig completion |

The decomposition writ IS the examination performed. Its content IS
the examiner's notes — the reasoning trace, the considered alternatives,
the rationale for the structural decisions made. Notes lives in
`writ.body`; the rig writes them on completion. Pre-completion, body
is empty/placeholder.

This dissolves the "kinda sucks — body is just `decompose vis-X-123`"
concern. The body becomes the substantive output of the rig, not a
trivial pointer.

### 5.4 Companion `DecompDoc` holds envelope metadata only

Following the cartograph precedent (VisionDoc/ChargeDoc/PieceDoc),
each decomposition writ has a typed companion doc keyed by writ id:

```typescript
interface DecompDoc {
  id: string;             // primary key — the decomposition writ id
  targetNodeId: string;   // the cartograph node being decomposed
  rigName: string;        // 'decomp-vision' | 'decomp-charge' | 'decomp-piece'
  rigVersion: string;     // for replay / diffing across rig versions
  decomposerId: string;   // which decomposer ran ('scaffold-decomposer' etc.)
  completedAt: string;    // ISO timestamp
  // notes are NOT here — they live in writ.body
}
```

Owned by the decomposer-apparatus substrate; shared across all
decomposer implementations. This is a deliberate narrowing from the
earlier design where the doc carried both metadata and notes — moving
notes to `writ.body` makes the writ first-class content and lets the
doc shrink to envelope.

### 5.5 Substrate + extension + default plugin shape

Three plugins, mirroring the Reckoner pattern:

1. **`@shardworks/cartograph-apparatus`** *(shipped)* — pure data layer.
   Vision/charge/piece writ types + companion docs + ladder-invariant
   API. Does not own any decomposition concept.

2. **`@shardworks/<decomposer>-apparatus`** *(new — substrate)*
   - Owns decomposition writ types and `books.<decomps>`
   - Owns the decomposer registry (kit-contribution surface)
   - Owns the CDC observer that fires on cartograph creates+updates
   - Owns the rig-name convention (`decomp-vision/charge/piece`)
   - Routes accepted decomposition petitions to the registered
     decomposer's rig templates
   - Stamps DecompDoc on completion (rig fills `writ.body`; substrate
     wraps the writ)
   - Provides the decomposer role-file convention
   - **Does not ship a concrete decomposer.** Substrate-only.

3. **`@shardworks/scaffold-<decomposer>`** *(new — default)*
   - Registers a decomposer with the substrate via kit contribution
   - Provides minimal rig templates for each cartograph layer
   - Useful immediately; designed to be replaced as approaches are
     iterated

Decomposing is implementation-uncertain territory — Sean has explicitly
said "we will need to experiment with approaches." The substrate
captures durable mechanics; the extension lets implementations evolve
freely.

### 5.6 Substrate watches cartograph CDC

The substrate's observer subscribes to:

- `book.cartograph.visions.created`
- `book.cartograph.visions.updated`
- `book.cartograph.charges.created`
- `book.cartograph.charges.updated`
- `book.cartograph.pieces.created`
- `book.cartograph.pieces.updated`

On any event, it creates the appropriate decomposition writ with
`parentId` pointing to the affected cartograph node, and stamps
`ext.reckoner` to enter the petition queue. The rig itself decides
whether the change warrants new structure — a typo-fix re-apply can
result in a decomposition rig whose notes say "no change warranted; no
children created."

Whether to gate on stage transitions (only fire when `stage: active`)
is open — see §6.2.

### 5.7 Spider's dispatchable type set extends

`c-moiu7yc1` originally concluded "mandates remain the dispatchable
unit (no need to extend dispatch to non-mandate writ types, even
though Spider's rigTemplateMappings would compose with that)." This
design needs to extend that — Spider's dispatchable set grows to
include decomposition writ types. The original conclusion explicitly
noted this would compose; the only reason to keep mandates as the sole
dispatchable type was "no need yet."

---

## 6. Open questions

### 6.1 Naming — the Surveyor collision

**This is the load-bearing question that gates several others.**

The recent rename (this conversation) renamed:
- `review-*` rigs → `survey-*` rigs
- `vision-keeper` role → `surveyor`
- The activity → "surveying"
- The substrate plugin → `surveyor-apparatus`
- The default → `scaffold-surveyor`

But the framework architecture **already reserves "Surveyor"** for the
codex-awareness apparatus. From `docs/guild-metaphor.md`:

> The Surveyor maintains the guild's knowledge of its codexes. When a
> codex is registered, the Surveyor inspects it — determining what
> kinds of work are applicable and how each is fulfilled for that
> specific codex.

And from `docs/architecture/index.md` line 27: the Surveyor is named
alongside Clerk and Clockworks as a core apparatus. From line 286: it
is "described as part of the guild's operational fabric, but [is] not
yet extracted as a standalone package."

**The collision is real.** The codex-Surveyor and our
cartograph-decomposer are conceptually adjacent (both produce
"knowledge of work-applicability over a substrate") but operate on
different substrates. Squinting at them as one apparatus blurs two
distinct concerns.

**Options:**

| Option | Move |
|---|---|
| (a) Keep "surveyor" for our thing; rename the codex-Surveyor (e.g., `Cartomancer`, `Lookout`, `Scout`) | Touches the architecture's reserved vocabulary; bigger blast radius |
| (b) Rename our thing back; leave codex-Surveyor as canonical | Re-rename right after the previous rename (churn) |
| (c) Disambiguate with prefixes (codex-surveyor, cartograph-surveyor) | Verbose but unambiguous |
| (d) Unify both under one Surveyor apparatus with multiple "domains" | Conflates two concerns; unclear win |

**My read:** (b) is the least bad. The codex-Surveyor concept is older
and load-bearing in the architecture; our naming pass landed in less
than a session and the alias registry already documents the rename so
re-renaming is cheap. The next-best name for our thing pairs with
`cartograph` directly: **Cartographer** (role) / **cartography**
(activity) / **chart** (output). The cartographer works the cartograph.

Rig names under "cartographer":
- `chart-vision`, `chart-charge`, `chart-piece` — short; the
  `chart-charge` c-c repetition Sean flagged earlier is real but
  livable
- `cartograph-vision/charge/piece` — verbose but unambiguous
- `decompose-vision/charge/piece` — bland, no register fit

**Sean's call. Until resolved, this doc uses "decomposer" /
"decomposition" as placeholders to avoid further entrenching either
name.**

### 6.2 Stage gating — should drafts trigger decomposition?

A vision lands at `stage: draft` per the cartograph CLI convention.
Current `nsg vision apply` design (per §5.1) presumably activates the
vision in the same call (or stage transitions are handled in the
sidecar). Two questions:

1. Should the substrate's observer fire on `stage: draft` events, or
   only on transitions to `stage: active`?
2. If gated on stage, what's the exact trigger — a stage transition
   event, or "stage is active at the moment we observe a create/update"?

**v0 lean:** every apply triggers a decomposition. If the patron wants
to draft without decomposing, they don't apply yet. Stage-gating layers
on later when draft-without-decomposing becomes a real workflow need.

**Risk:** patron does a series of edits + applies during initial
authoring; each apply triggers a decomposition petition. Reckoner
handles the queue but resource costs accrue. Maybe we want the
stage-active gate from day one.

### 6.3 Dynamic rig template registration (`c-moje41iq`)

Spider currently resolves rigs via static `rigTemplateMappings`
configured by writ type. Concrete decomposer implementations need to
register rig templates for `decomp-vision/charge/piece` (or whatever
the typed names become) at plugin load time.

Four candidate mechanisms:

1. **Dynamic kit-contribution registration** — plugin's `supportKit`
   declares rig templates; Spider aggregates static config + contributed
   templates at startup. Matches the Reckoner-scheduler pattern. **Most
   natural fit.**
2. **Static config with templating** — parameter substitution. Poor
   fit; decomposers want fundamentally different rigs, not parameter
   variations.
3. **Rig 'include' composition** — useful for shared substrate
   boilerplate (DecompDoc stamping, parent-reading) but doesn't solve
   the registration question on its own.
4. **Engine grafting / graftTailing** — substrate provides one universal
   decomposition rig; decomposer contributes engines that get grafted at
   runtime. Higher substrate share; more complex.

**Prerequisite:** audit Spider's existing rig-resolution mechanism. We
need to know how it discriminates between rigs when multiple writ
"kinds" exist (implementer/spec/reviewer/decomposer would all
potentially be different rigs over different writ types). The
dispatchable-set extension from §5.7 needs this audit before it can be
designed concretely.

### 6.4 Default `scaffold-<decomposer>` design (`c-moje4dj9`)

What does the default decomposer actually do?

- One rig template per cartograph layer, or one recursive template that
  handles all three?
- Model selection (sonnet vs opus vs other)?
- Prompt structure — what does the rig actually read and write?
- Stop conditions: when does the rig decide to stop creating children
  vs decompose further?
- Re-decomposition (drift) — does the rig know it's a re-run vs first
  pass? Does it diff against prior decomposition writs for the same
  target node?

**v0 lean:** three layer-specific templates (matches Spider's
per-writ-type dispatch model from the audit); basic LLM prompts; no
stop conditions other than "create children if you think the parent
warrants further breakdown, otherwise complete with notes explaining
why no children were warranted"; re-runs are stateless (rig reads
prior decomposition writs as context if it wants).

### 6.5 Substrate scope detail (`c-moje49pk`)

Where exactly does the substrate / decomposer-implementation boundary
fall?

| Capability | Substrate? | Decomposer impl? |
|---|---|---|
| CDC observer for cartograph book changes | Yes | No |
| Initial decomposition petition emission | Yes | No |
| Rig-template routing (writ type → template id) | Yes (registry-based) | No |
| Actual rig template (prompts, model, tools) | No | Yes |
| DecompDoc stamping on rig completion | Yes | No |
| Default priority-dimension scheme | Yes (overridable) | Optional override |
| Stop conditions / decomposition heuristics | No | Yes |
| Source-id grammar for ext.reckoner | Yes (default) | Optional override |
| Role-file convention | Yes | Optional override |

**Goal:** push as much as possible into substrate so concrete
decomposers are minimal kit contributions. This table is a starting
proposal — Sean's review/revision welcome.

### 6.6 Plural decomposers (`c-moje4foc`, currently parked)

When multiple decomposer implementations are registered with the
substrate, how does the substrate select which one to dispatch for a
given decomposition petition? Per-vision config? Per-cartograph-tree?
Round-robin? Default-with-override?

**Not for v0** — only one decomposer registered. Parked.

---

## 7. Recommended commission sequence

These can dispatch as soon as naming (§6.1) is resolved. Each is small
enough to commission as a single brief.

### Commission A — Vision authoring on disk

- `nsg vision apply <slug>` CLI verb (single code path for first/Nth
  import)
- Sidecar schema (`vision-metadata.yml` with `visionId`, stage, codex)
- Stale-binding detection (warn at scan, error on apply)
- Tests: import a fresh vision, re-import after edits, error on stale
  binding

Ships in the cartograph-apparatus package; no new plugin required.

### Commission B — Decomposer-apparatus substrate

- New plugin `@shardworks/<decomposer>-apparatus`
- Decomposition writ types (one or three — settle in commission brief
  per §6.4)
- DecompDoc + `books.<decomps>` companion book
- Decomposer registry (kit-contribution surface)
- CDC observer on cartograph book events; creates decomposition writs
- Rig-template routing (substrate looks up registered decomposer's
  templates)
- DecompDoc stamping on rig completion
- Tests: CDC fires → writ created → ext.reckoner stamped

Depends on resolution of §6.3 (dynamic rig-template registration) for
the routing piece — that may need a small Spider PR first.

### Commission C — Spider extension for decomposition writ dispatch

- Audit Spider's current rig-resolution mechanism (§6.3 prerequisite)
- Extend dispatchable-type set to include decomposition writ types
- Template lookup integrates with the substrate's registry
- Tests: a typed decomposition writ in `open` phase dispatches the
  registered decomposer's rig

Could fold into Commission B or stand alone. Standalone if the audit
reveals Spider work that's worth scoping separately.

### Commission D — Scaffold-decomposer default

- New plugin `@shardworks/scaffold-<decomposer>`
- Three rig templates (vision, charge, piece) — basic LLM prompts
- Registers with the substrate via kit contribution
- Tests: end-to-end smoke test (vision applied → decomposition writ
  created → rig dispatched → charges created → recursion fires)

Depends on B (substrate exists) and C (Spider can dispatch). This is
the first-light commission for the whole cascade.

### Order of operations

```
A (vision apply) ─┐
                  ├─→ E2E ready
B (substrate) ────┤
C (Spider) ───────┤
D (default) ──────┘
```

A is independent of the others. B/C/D have dependencies as written. A
clean dispatch order: A first (it's smallest and ships value
immediately even before decomposition lands), then B+C in either order
(small audit needed for C), then D last.

---

## 8. Proposed click tree (replacing c-moa42rxh subtree)

The current `c-moa42rxh` subtree has 22 children, many concluded but
some live with overlapping concerns. After this synthesis lands, a
cleaner tree could look like:

```
c-NEW-ROOT  Decomposition cascade — patron-vision authoring + cartograph
            decomposition + Reckoner-gated dispatch [live]
├── c-NEW-A  Vision authoring on disk (commission A)               [live]
├── c-NEW-B  Decomposer-apparatus substrate (commission B)         [live]
│   ├── c-NEW-B1  Substrate scope detail (was c-moje49pk)          [live]
│   ├── c-NEW-B2  Stage gating policy (§6.2)                       [live]
│   └── c-NEW-B3  Decomposition writ shape — one type or three     [live]
├── c-NEW-C  Spider rig-resolution audit + extension (commission C) [live]
│   └── c-NEW-C1  Dynamic rig-template registration (was c-moje41iq) [live]
├── c-NEW-D  Default scaffold-decomposer (commission D)            [live]
│   └── c-NEW-D1  Rig template shape per layer (was c-moje4dj9)    [live]
├── c-NEW-Z  Naming — Surveyor collision (§6.1, gates everything)  [live]
└── c-NEW-Y  Plural decomposers — future                           [parked]

[Concluded historical clicks parked under an "archive" branch:
 c-mod53o6h (cartograph data scaffold + 5 children, all shipped),
 c-mod53ood, c-mod53q2r, c-modee*, c-moeplgm5, c-moerc0vf,
 c-moivk7pd subtree, c-moivk8yc, c-moivk9qw, c-moivkc4y,
 c-moivkfgb, c-moiu7yc1, c-moiu8pm9, c-moiu8tm4, c-moje45l8,
 c-moje41iq, c-moje49pk, c-moje4dj9, c-moje4foc]
```

The reorg moves from "design questions about a vision-keeper concept"
to "commissions and their open questions." The old design questions
either resolved (concluded) or got absorbed into commission scope.

---

The clicks this synthesis draws from. Concluded conclusions are the

content.text:
Drafted at `.scratch/cartograph-design-synthesis.md` (~590 lines). Here's a high-level read of what I found and what the doc says.

## Key findings worth surfacing before you read

**1. Naming collision — biggest landmine.** The framework architecture already reserves "Surveyor" for the codex-awareness apparatus (per `guild-metaphor.md` and `architecture/index.md`). The rename we just landed walks straight into it. The doc flags this as the gating open question (§6.1) and uses **"decomposer"** as a placeholder throughout to avoid further entrenching either name. My read is that re-renaming our thing is cheaper than touching the architecture's reserved vocabulary — Cartographer/cartography pairs naturally with the cartograph apparatus.

**2. The shipped `vision-keeper` plugin isn't what you think.** It's a Reckoner-contract worked-example that submits drift snapshots — it doesn't survey, decompose, or run rigs. The plugin's existence is a conceptual landmine: anyone reading the code sees `vision-keeper` and assumes it's the apparatus we've been designing. The doc is explicit about this in §2.4 and recommends a sanctum-side rename (e.g., `vision-keeper-example`) to dissolve the confusion.

**3. The design is mostly solid.** Once naming resolves, four clean commissions are ready (§7):
- A: Vision authoring on disk (independent, ships value immediately)
- B: Decomposer-apparatus substrate
- C: Spider extension for new dispatchable writ types (small audit prerequisite)
- D: Scaffold-decomposer default

## Doc structure

- **§1** — End-to-end picture (flow + concept map)
- **§2** — What's shipped, with commit references and a clarifier on the vision-keeper landmine
- **§3** — In-flight (nothing right now)
- **§4** — Not built (most of the cascade)
- **§5** — Solid design ready to commission (7 sub-decisions, each cross-referenced to its source clicks)
- **§6** — Open questions (6, with the naming collision first as the gate)
- **§7** — Recommended commission sequence
- **§8** — Proposed new click tree (flat replacement for the c-moa42rxh subtree)
- **Appendix** — Source clicks indexed for traceability

## Refined design moves (§5)

Three things sharpened during the synthesis vs where we landed earlier in the chat:

- **§5.3** — Decomposition writ's `body` is the rig's notes (set on completion). DecompDoc shrinks to envelope-only metadata. Dissolves the "trivial body" complaint by treating decomposition as a ladder-writ-style content carrier rather than a mandate-style instruction carrier.
- **§5.7** — Spider's dispatchable-set extension is part of the design now, not a separate amendment. The supersede of `c-moiu7yc1`'s "mandates only" decision folds into the substrate commission.
- **§7** — Commission A (disk authoring) can land independently of B/C/D. That decoupling means we ship something useful without waiting for the substrate work.

## Two requests as you read

1. **The naming question (§6.1) is load-bearing.** Resolve it first — several other open questions are blocked behind it. I lean toward Cartographer for our thing, but I want your read more than my recommendation here.

2. **§8's proposed click tree is a sketch.** I made it minimal on purpose — flatten the live-design noise into commission-shaped clicks plus their open sub-questions. If you want a different shape (e.g., keep the existing concluded-history visible, or further subdivide some commissions), it's easier to show me the shape than for me to guess.

When you've read it, ping me and we can decide whether to commit the scratch doc as-is for working, refine inline, or move it to a permanent location once it stabilizes.

content: We're redesigning the cartograph decomposition system end-to-end; I just drafted a synthesis doc at `.scratch/cartograph-design-synthesis.md` covering shipped state, solid design, and open questions. Next: you read it and resolve the naming collision in §6.1.

content.text:
Solid questions, taking them one at a time.

## 1. Survey-as-child writs (yes), not rigs-bound-to-cartograph-nodes

The decomposition writ is a child writ that sits **alongside** the cartograph nodes it helped create — not bound directly to the cartograph node itself.

```
vision vis-1                                        ← cartograph node (long-lived)
├── decomp-vis-1   [decomposition writ, terminal]   ← rig's notes here, in writ.body
├── charge ch-1   [from decomposition]              ← cartograph node
├── charge ch-2
└── charge ch-3
    ├── decomp-ch-3  [terminal]                     ← decomposition of ch-3
    ├── piece pc-1
    └── piece pc-2
```

Why not bind the rig directly to the vision writ:

- A vision sits in `phase: open` for its entire lifetime. Spider's "dispatch when in dispatchable phase" model would fire once and never again. Re-firing on content changes requires custom machinery the rest of the framework doesn't have.
- "Do this once" semantics (decomposition completes, writ terminates) belong on a writ separate from "exist persistently" (the vision). Mixing them on one writ requires inventing rig-attempt history fields the cartograph types don't have.
- Each decomposition is a distinct unit of work with its own Reckoner gating, its own notes, its own outcome. Distinct writs naturally express that.

So: each decomposition is a separate writ. It comes into existence when the substrate observer fires, lives long enough for the rig to do its work, and reaches a terminal state. Multiple decompositions of the same vision (initial + re-decomp on edits) are multiple writs over time.

## 2. Edits to charges/pieces — supersedes, not in-place edits

**No direct editing of charges or pieces.** They're rig-produced, not patron-authored, and they're durable structural records. The substrate's mechanism for "this charge needs to change" is **supersedes** — the rig creates a new charge with a `supersedes: <oldChargeId>` link, marking the old one superseded.

I'd skip explicit "v2 / v3" naming. The supersedes chain *is* the version history — walk supersedes-of links to get ancestry. Adding explicit version numbers in titles or doc fields buys nothing the chain doesn't already give us, and creates the awkward question of whether "small edit = version bump" or "structural change = new charge entirely."

What that looks like end-to-end:

1. Patron edits `vision.md` and runs `nsg vision apply <slug>`.
2. CDC fires on `book.cartograph.visions.updated`.
3. Substrate creates a fresh `decomp-vision` writ.
4. Rig reads:
   - The (updated) vision body
   - **The existing charges** (not just the vision in isolation — re-decomposition needs to see what's already there)
5. Rig reasons about delta and decides per-charge:
   - **Keep** — existing charge is still right; do nothing
   - **Supersede** — vision change makes this charge wrong; create replacement, link `supersedes`
   - **Add** — vision change introduces new structure not previously captured; create new charge
   - **(Soft)** drop — superseded charges with no replacement; rig creates a `cancellation` charge or the rig transitions the old charge to `cancelled` stage with reasoning in its notes
6. Rig completes; its notes explain every decision.

Same logic recurses to pieces. The patron-contract boundary at the charge layer means superseding a charge is a *visible* event — patron walkthrough surfaces "ch-3 was replaced because the vision now emphasizes Y instead of X." Pieces aren't patron-facing so the same mechanism is invisible to the patron unless they look.

### Substrate vs surveyor split

| Substrate codifies | Surveyor implementations decide |
|---|---|
| `supersedes` link kind exists and is queryable | Whether to supersede vs keep |
| `cartograph.createCharge({ supersedes })` API supports the link atomically | What threshold of vision-change triggers supersede |
| Re-decomposition rigs receive existing children as input context (substrate-provided) | How aggressively to rewrite vs conserve |
| Superseded charges remain queryable; lifecycle continues for their dispatched work | Whether superseding cascades to children (drop pieces of a superseded charge?) |
| The `decomp-X` writ records the rig's decision in its notes | Stop conditions, reasoning approach |

The substrate doesn't impose policy. It provides the mechanism (supersedes link, cartograph API, re-decomp context-provision) and lets surveyor implementations make judgment calls.

**Cascade question worth flagging:** when ch-3 is superseded by ch-3-new, what happens to ch-3's pieces? Substrate-level default I'd recommend: **pieces stay attached to the superseded charge**; their lifecycle is independent. The surveyor's notes can call out "ch-3-new should have these pieces re-attached" if it wants — but the substrate doesn't auto-cascade. This avoids destructive automatic moves; the patron walkthrough surfaces the orphaned-pieces case for resolution.

## 3. Naming — Surveyor wins; codex-mapping concept yields

Got it. The move:

- Update `guild-metaphor.md` to redefine The Surveyor as the cartograph-decomposition apparatus (the role we just designed)
- Remove "codex-mapping" framing from `architecture/index.md` and `architecture/plugins.md`
- Update the alias registry to capture the supersession (the prior codex-Surveyor concept is now subsumed; if a codex-awareness apparatus is later needed, it'll need a different name)
- Restore "surveyor" / "surveying" / "survey-vision" / etc. as the canonical names through this design (drop the "decomposer" placeholder I used in the synthesis doc)

I'll do this as part of the cleanup commit alongside the vision-keeper-apparatus deletion.

## 4. Delete the existing `vision-keeper-apparatus` plugin

Yes. The Reckoner-contract worked example doesn't justify its naming-collision-and-confusion cost. The contract document at `docs/architecture/petitioner-registration.md` is the load-bearing reference; an in-tree example can be reborn later if needed (and could be built into something genuinely useful — e.g., an exemplar petitioner with cleaner illustrative semantics, or fold into a sentinel example).

Removing it touches:
- `packages/plugins/vision-keeper/` (whole dir)
- Any `guild.json` that includes it
- Any Clockworks standing orders referencing `vision-keeper-on-decline`
- The petitioner-registration doc's references to it as a worked example
- Tests that import it

This is a framework-side change — needs to be commissioned out (sanctum can't reach into the framework repo to do deletions cleanly). I can draft that brief alongside the survey-substrate work.

## 5. Substrate-level priority defaults — yes

Sensible global values do exist. Looking at the vision-keeper plugin's existing dimension defaults as precedent (it's the canonical worked example for how a petitioner stamps these), here's what survey-driven petitions should default to:

| Dimension | Substrate default | Why this default | Surveyor override? |
|---|---|---|---|
| `visionRelation` | `vision-advancer` | Surveys produce structure that advances the vision toward implementable work | Could escalate to `vision-violator` if a re-survey detects existing structure contradicts the new vision |
| `severity` | `moderate` | Routine surveys aren't critical; they're scheduled work | Yes — surveyor can pick `serious` for re-surveys triggered by major vision drift |
| `scope` | layer-based: `major-area` for vision, `minor-area` for charge, `trivial` for piece | Each layer is naturally smaller-scoped | Yes |
| `time` | `{ decay: false, deadline: null }` | Surveys aren't time-pressured — they sit until guild capacity allows | Yes (surveyor could set deadline if e.g. patron needs a specific charge surveyed before an event) |
| `domain` | `['planning']` | Surveys produce structural/planning artifacts, not feature code or quality work | Surveyor's choice; multi-domain plausible |
| `complexity` | `bounded` | One survey is one rig run; cascading layers are separate writs | Yes (e.g. `unbounded` if a particular surveyor uses long-running reasoning) |

**Patron-level priority hints can layer on via VisionDoc fields.** A `priority` field on the vision (or per-charge on ChargeDoc) lets the patron say "this vision is high-priority for the product" — substrate reads that when emitting petitions and adjusts the defaults. That's a small VisionDoc/ChargeDoc extension, easy and additive.

The pattern then is: substrate-defaults + VisionDoc-hint-overrides + surveyor-implementation-overrides, layered in that order. Surveyors that want to be smart can be; surveyors that don't care get sensible behavior for free.

## Where this leaves the synthesis doc

Three updates needed before this becomes the working design:

1. Restore "surveyor / surveying / survey-*" naming throughout (drop the "decomposer" placeholder)
2. Add §5.8: Edits-and-supersedes — the section above
3. Add §5.9: Substrate priority defaults — the table above
4. Update §6 to remove the naming-collision question (resolved) and the v0 vision-keeper-plugin landmine (will be deleted)

Want me to drive those edits and re-flow the doc, plus draft the brief for deleting the existing vision-keeper plugin? I can also do the architecture-doc updates (Surveyor redefinition) at the same time so the framework's own docs stop pointing at the codex-mapping concept.
